Documentation for adding and serving static files like CSS in a Flask application.

# Purpose
The content describes the use and management of static files in a Flask web application. Static files, such as CSS, JavaScript, and images, are stored in the `flaskr/static` directory and are served by Flask using a built-in `static` view. The `base.html` template includes a link to a `style.css` file, which is used to style the HTML layout. The document provides a sample CSS code block to be copied into the `flaskr/static/style.css` file, which defines styles for various HTML elements to enhance the visual appearance of the application. The content also advises on refreshing the browser or clearing the cache if changes to static files do not appear immediately.
# Content Summary
The document provides instructions for managing static files in a Flask web application, specifically focusing on adding CSS to style HTML templates. Static files, such as CSS, JavaScript, and images, are stored in the `flaskr/static` directory. Flask automatically serves these files using a `static` view, which can be accessed with the `url_for('static', filename='...')` function.

The document includes a CSS code snippet to be copied into the `flaskr/static/style.css` file. This CSS code styles various HTML elements, such as `html`, `body`, `h1`, `a`, `nav`, and others, to enhance the visual appearance of the authentication views and templates. The CSS styles include font settings, background colors, padding, and layout adjustments.

Developers are instructed to visit the URL `http://127.0.0.1:5000/auth/login` to view the styled login page. If changes are made to static files, developers should refresh the browser page to see updates. If changes do not appear, clearing the browser's cache may be necessary. The document also provides a link to Mozilla's CSS documentation for further reading.
